evidence: Brackish resonates most with sensory perception (taste), identification as a specific salt-mix term, and lived experience of tasting it. It operates at material/relational layers (physical mixtures) and in ecosystems (e.g., estuaries). Its inclusion of salt and fresh water aligns with symmetry.inclusive, and its dependence on environmental conditions scores lawfulness.contingent. Discovery of brackishness and its functional role in ecosystems add further resonance.

Briny strongly resonates with sensory perception (10) and material existence (10), as it directly describes a tangible salt taste. It also ties to essential resonance (10) as the core essence of saltiness. Functional value (5) reflects brine's practical uses like preservation. Predictable lawfulness (7) arises from its consistent association with salt presence. Lower scores reflect its lack of broader systemic or existential implications.

Briny strongly resonates with material existence (ontological.material 10) and direct sensory perception (epistemological.perception 10). It is tied to the essential nature of salt's presence (resonance.essential 7) and predictable when salt is involved (lawfulness.predictable 7). The taste operates at an individual scale (scale.individual 5) and is independent of other factors (interconnection.independent 5). Its aesthetic value (6) and functional role in preservation (5) also stand out.

The highest scores reflect 'fresh' as a sensory (epistemological.perception) and material (ontological.material) quality tied to saltiness, its surface-level nature (resonance.superficial), and its capacity to generate new taste experiences (symmetry.generative). Lower scores acknowledge weak ties to individual scale, predictable patterns, and aesthetic value.

The term 'main' in the context of PER.salty (saltiness) strongly resonates with the physical nature of salt (ontological.material), the sensory experience of tasting salt (epistemological.perception), and the core essence of the trait (resonance.essential). It also reflects a predictable, fundamental characteristic (lawfulness.predictable) and contributes to aesthetic appreciation (value_generation.aesthetic). The destructive symmetry score acknowledges saltiness as a potentially negative or excessive trait.

Piquant resonates most strongly with sensory perception (epistemological.perception), individual experience (scale.individual), and the essential quality of taste (resonance.essential). It slightly influences temporal aspects (indication/influence) and connects to transformative taste dynamics (symmetry.transformative). Its aesthetic value (value_generation.aesthetic) and experiential comprehension (experience.comprehension) highlight its role in enhancing sensory appreciation without overlapping with the symmetric pair PER.sweet.

Saliferous strongly resonates with sensory perception (taste) and material existence. Its identification and experiential qualities align with direct sensory engagement. Essential resonance reflects salt's core role in flavor. Symmetry scores reflect both constructive (flavor enhancement, preservation) and destructive (excess harm) dynamics. Functional and aesthetic values arise from practical utility and sensory appeal.

Salinate strongly resonates with material, essential resonance, and predictability because it directly alters physical taste, core to PER.salty's essence, and reliably produces saltiness. Scores drop where effects are non-physical, non-causal, or unrelated to taste dynamics.

Saline strongly resonates with sensory perception (direct taste), physical materiality (salt's tangibility), and essential resonance (core essence of saltiness). It scores high in predictability (consistent taste) and functional/aesthetic value (practical uses and flavor enhancement). Lower scores reflect its limited scale (individual experience) and conditional effects (contingent on quantity).

Saline strongly resonates with sensory perception (epistemological.perception 10) and material existence (ontological.material 10). It connects to taste experience (epistemological.experience 8) and aesthetic value (value_generation.aesthetic 7). Its essential resonance (resonance.essential 8) ties to the core definition of saltiness. Lower scores reflect its limited scale (individual) and conditional presence (lawfulness.contingent 4).

Saline solution strongly resonates with its physical existence (ontological.material) and the essential quality of saltiness (resonance.essential). It also aligns with functional utility (value_generation.functional), sensory perception (epistemological.perception), and independent existence (interconnection.independent). Lower scores reflect its lack of deep connections to other slots beyond its core attributes.

Salinity as 'PER.salty' resonates strongly with sensory perception (direct taste), semantic meaning (core essence of saltiness), individual experience (personal attribute), and aesthetic value (sensory appreciation). It is predictable (lawful) and operates independently (not dependent on others). Weak temporal connections exist in emergence/continuation of the taste.

Salt's resonance peaks in sensory perception (10), individual experience (10), and material existence (10). Its identification as 'salty' (8) and aesthetic appreciation (8) reflect its core role in taste. The taste's independence (8) and essential resonance (8) highlight its foundational nature, while predictability (10) underscores its expected sensory role. Lower scores reflect its lack of systemic or existential depth beyond direct sensory engagement.

Saltiness strongly resonates with sensory perception (epistemological.perception 10) and material existence (ontological.material 10). It operates at an individual scale (scale.individual 10) and has aesthetic value (value.aesthetic 7). Its essential resonance (resonance.essential 8) reflects its core sensory role, while its catalytic effect (interconnection.catalytic 4) and predictable nature (lawfulness.predictable 9) further define its presence.

Saltish resonates most with sensory perception (epistemological.perception 7) and material basis (ontological.material 8). Its minor deviation from 'salty' creates a superficial resonance (6) and predictable nature (lawfulness.predictable 5). Aesthetic value (3) arises from sensory appeal, while its relation to the symmetric pair 'sweet' keeps destructive symmetry low (2).

The word 'salty' strongly resonates with physical/material existence (ontological.material) and individual sensory experience (scale.individual, epistemological.perception/experience). Its essential resonance (resonance.essential) ties to core taste perception. Functional utility (value_generation.functional) and aesthetic enhancement (value_generation.aesthetic) reflect salt's practical and sensory roles. Predictable manifestation (lawfulness.predictable) aligns with its consistent sensory profile.

Stimulating resonates most with salt's role in enhancing flavor (catalytic), its material presence (material), and its transformative effect on taste. Over-salting (destructive) and preservation (permanence) highlight its dual impact. Predictable effects and functional value (preservation) further align with salt's essential role in culinary contexts.

The highest scores reflect 'tasty' as a sensory experience (perception:10), tied to physical salt (material:10), and valued aesthetically (aesthetic:10). It resonates with essential taste qualities (resonance.essential:8) and semantic meaning (semantic:8). Lower scores reflect its role in embodied knowing (experience:7), predictable taste recognition (lawfulness.predictable:7), and weak generative or catalytic connections.
